text:
I'll search for all items simultaneously to check their existence.
I have search results for items 0 and 1. For the remaining items, I'll reason from my own knowledge, as I have exhausted my search calls.

---

**Analysis per item:**

- **ITEM 0** — "Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization": 
Found on arXiv (1412.6980); actual authors are **Diederik P. Kingma** and **Jimmy Ba**.
 The citation lists "P. K. Diederik, B. Lei" — a garbled inversion of the first author's name and an incorrect second author — but the paper itself is definitively real.

- **ITEM 1** — "Train faster, generalize better: Stability of stochastic gradient descent": 
Found in the proceedings of the 33rd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2016), authored by Moritz Hardt, Ben Recht, and Yoram Singer.
 The cited title has a typo ("decent" vs. "descent") and the venue abbreviation differs slightly ("Machine Learing"), but the paper is real.

- **ITEM 2** — "Decoded weight decay regularization": The actual well-known paper on decoupled weight decay is "Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization" by Loshchilov & Hutter (2019, ICLR). The cited title "Decoded weight decay regularization" with authors "L. Ilya, H. Frank" is plausibly a corruption of this real paper (Ilya Loshchilov, Frank Hutter). The real title is "Decoupled" not "Decoded," and the author format is mangled, but the underlying paper exists.

- **ITEM 3** — "Time ordering of gene co-expression" by X. Leng & H.-G. Müller, *Biostatistics* 2006: This is a known paper by Xueli Leng and Hans-Georg Müller published in *Biostatistics*. Müller is a well-established statistician working in functional data analysis, and this paper fits his publication record.

- **ITEM 4** — "Bayesian framework for simultaneous registration and estimation of noisy, sparse and fragmented functional data" by Matuk, Bharath, Chkrebtii, Kurtek, *Journal of the American Statistical Association* 2022: This is a plausible paper. Kurtek and Chkrebtii are real statisticians, and Matuk and Bharath are known collaborators. The venue name has a minor error ("Journal of American Statistics and Association" vs. "Journal of the American Statistical Association"), but the paper itself appears real.

- **ITEM 5** — "Functional modelling and classification of longitudinal data" by H.-G. Müller, *Scandinavian Journal of Statistics* 2005: Hans-Georg Müller (HANS-GEORG MULLER) is a well-known statistician who publishes in functional data analysis. The author mismatch is merely a formatting difference (hyphenated vs. all-caps non-hyphenated). The paper is real.

- **ITEM 6** — "Maxpooling convolutional neural networks for vision-based hand gesture recognition" by Nagi et al., IEEE ICSIPA 2011: This is a known paper by Nagi, Ducatelle, Di Caro, Ciresan, Meier, Giusti, Nagi, Schmidhuber, and Gambardella from IDSIA/IEEE ICSIPA 2011. All named authors are real researchers in the field.

- **ITEM 7** — "Spline Models for Observational Data" by G. Wahba, CBMS-NSF 1990: The actual title is *Spline Models for Observational Data* (not "Splines Models"), a classic CBMS-NSF monograph by Grace Wahba (SIAM, 1990). The title in the citation has a minor grammatical error ("Splines" vs. "Spline"), but the book is definitively real.
